speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Kind uncle York, the latest news we hear

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Is that the rebels have consumed with fire

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Our town of Ciceter in Gloucestershire,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But whether they be ta’en or slain we hear not.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Welcome, my lord. What is the news?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: First, to thy sacred state wish I all happiness.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The next news is: I have to London sent

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The heads of Oxford, Salisbury, Blunt, and Kent.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The manner of their taking may appear

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: At large discoursèd in this paper here.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We thank thee, gentle Percy, for thy pains,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And to thy worth will add right worthy gains.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: My lord, I have from Oxford sent to London

speaker: FITZWATER
text: The heads of Brocas and Sir Bennet Seely,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: Two of the dangerous consorted traitors

speaker: FITZWATER
text: That sought at Oxford thy dire overthrow.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thy pains, Fitzwater, shall not be forgot.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Right noble is thy merit, well I wot.

speaker: PERCY
text: The grand conspirator, Abbot of Westminster,

speaker: PERCY
text: With clog of conscience and sour melancholy

speaker: PERCY
text: Hath yielded up his body to the grave.

speaker: PERCY
text: But here is Carlisle living, to abide

speaker: PERCY
text: Thy kingly doom and sentence of his pride.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Carlisle, this is your doom:

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Choose out some secret place, some reverend room,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: More than thou hast, and with it joy thy life.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: So, as thou liv’st in peace, die free from strife;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For, though mine enemy thou hast ever been,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: High sparks of honor in thee have I seen.

speaker: EXTON
text: Great king, within this coffin I present

speaker: EXTON
text: Thy buried fear. Herein all breathless lies

speaker: EXTON
text: The mightiest of thy greatest enemies,

speaker: EXTON
text: Richard of Bourdeaux, by me hither brought.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Exton, I thank thee not, for thou hast wrought

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A deed of slander with thy fatal hand

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Upon my head and all this famous land.

speaker: EXTON
text: From your own mouth, my lord, did I this deed.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: They love not poison that do poison need,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor do I thee. Though I did wish him dead,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I hate the murderer, love him murderèd.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The guilt of conscience take thou for thy labor,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But neither my good word nor princely favor.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With Cain go wander through shades of night,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And never show thy head by day nor light.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lords, I protest my soul is full of woe

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That blood should sprinkle me to make me grow.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come mourn with me for what I do lament,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And put on sullen black incontinent.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I’ll make a voyage to the Holy Land

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To wash this blood off from my guilty hand.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: March sadly after. Grace my mournings here

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In weeping after this untimely bier.
